meta.title: Regulator-Resilient Enterprise AGI/ASI Governance Architecture for Fortune 500 / Global 2000 / G-SIFIs (2026-2030)
meta.subtitle: Board-grade synthesis combining EU AI Act + Basel III + ISO/IEC 42001 + NIST AI RMF, three-lines-of-defense execution, supervisory interrogation packs, frontier AGI containment, predictive governance, an autonomous React Governance Command Center, the Joint Supervisory Operating Protocol (JSOP), and the Supervisory Codex Charter — a self-verifying, regulator-integrated, temporally continuous governance system with embedded cultural persistence and multi-modal evidence integrity.
meta.classification: CONFIDENTIAL — Board / Prudential Supervisor / SOC / Treaty Authority / AI Safety Institute
meta.owner: Group CRO + Chief AI Officer (CAIO) + CISO — co-signed by CCO, GC, DPO, Head of Internal Audit; Board Chair attests quarterly
audience: Board of Directors / Risk Committee / Audit Committee / Ethics Committee
Executive Committee (CEO, CFO, CRO, CCO, CISO, CAIO, CTO, COO)
Prudential supervisors (ECB SSM, Federal Reserve, PRA, OCC, MAS, HKMA)
AI Safety Institutes (UK AISI, US AISI, EU AI Office)
G7 Hiroshima Process Code of Conduct signatories
Internal Audit (3rd LoD), Group Compliance, MRM (2nd LoD)

subjectSystem.scopeOfAi: All AI systems — narrow ML, generative LLMs, agentic AI, frontier foundation models, and any system approaching AGI capability tier T4+
anchorUseCases: AI-CR-UNDERWRITE-01 (high-risk credit, EU AI Act Annex III §5(b))
AGI-TRADER-PROD-01 (algorithmic trading, EU AI Act Art. 53/55)
FRONTIER-FM-01 (frontier foundation model, internal capability T4)
subjectSystem.scale: 25+ jurisdictions · 1,500+ AI systems · 400+ models in production · up to 3 frontier foundation models with compute budget > 10^25 FLOPs

regulatoryAlignment: Basel III/IV — CRR3/CRD6 — Pillar 1 + Pillar 2 ICAAP AI capital overlay
ISO/IEC 42001:2023 — AI Management System (AIMS) — primary anchor
NIST AI RMF 1.0 + GenAI Profile (AI 600-1) + AI 800-218 (SSDF)
ECB SSM Guide on internal models (2024) + TRIM AI extensions
Federal Reserve SR 11-7 / OCC 2011-12 — Model Risk Management
FCRA §604/§615 + ECOA Reg B + CFPB Circular 2023-03
OECD AI Principles + G7 Hiroshima AI Process Code of Conduct
Council of Europe Framework Convention on AI (2024)
EO 14110 §4.2 + OMB M-24-10 + UK AI Safety Summit Bletchley/Seoul commitments
OWASP LLM Top 10 (2025) / MITRE ATLAS / NIST CSF 2.0
SLSA L3 + Sigstore/Cosign + in-toto + Rekor + SEC 17a-4(f)

executiveSummary.purpose: Provide boards, regulators and supervisors a single, self-verifying, multi-modal evidence framework that makes enterprise AI — including frontier AGI/ASI systems — regulator-resilient through 2030 and continuity-assured beyond.
executiveSummary.thesis: Regulator resilience requires three properties: (1) machine-verifiable truthfulness of every governance claim; (2) temporal continuity across regulator changes, model regenerations, and incidents; (3) cultural persistence so the institution's risk posture survives executive turnover.
designPrinciples: Regulator-by-design: every artefact assembles into a JSOP filing
Self-verifying: every claim cryptographically reproducible from telemetry
Predictive: forecast control breaches before they manifest
Multi-modal evidence: text, telemetry, artefact, attestation, ritual
Cultural persistence: the Codex outlives any single executive
Frontier-aware: AGI/ASI tier T4+ trigger automatic capability gates
Cross-jurisdiction first-class: drift reconciled across home + host regulators
executiveSummary.boardNarrative: By 2030 our AI estate is regulator-resilient: every decision is reproducible, every control is enforced as code, every obligation is mechanically checked, and the supervisory compact is renewed via cryptographic ritual. The institution's AI risk culture is no longer dependent on any individual — it is inscribed.

M1_boardOversight.title: M1 — Board Oversight & Executive Accountability (CAIO / CRO / CISO)
M1_boardOversight.summary: Board-grade governance, accountabilities, and committee architecture.

sections.title: Board AI Oversight Committee (charter)
charter: Approve AI Policy + Risk Appetite Statement (RAS) annually
Receive quarterly KPI pack + ad-hoc Sev-0/Sev-1 attestations
Approve Tier-1 model risk thresholds + frontier capability gates
Sign Supervisory Codex annually; co-sign JSOP filings
Authorise AI capital overlay (Basel III/IV Pillar 2)
composition: Members: 2 NEDs + Chief Risk Officer + AI Ethics external advisor
Standing attendees: CAIO, CCO, CISO, DPO, Head of Internal Audit

M2_regulatoryAlignment.title: M2 — Regulatory Alignment Matrix (EU AI Act + Basel III + ISO 42001 + NIST AI RMF)
M2_regulatoryAlignment.summary: Unified mapping that assembles a single control once and projects it into every regulator overlay.

sections.title: ISO/IEC 42001 AIMS + NIST AI RMF in CI/CD + telemetry
ciCdHooks: Pre-commit: prompt + dataset lint + DPIA freshness check
Pre-merge: model card completeness + eval coverage + SBOM
Pre-deploy: OPA bundle conformance + signed model attestation (in-toto)
Post-deploy: telemetry envelope sample + canary fairness/drift watch
Quarterly: AIMS internal audit + NIST RMF re-mapping CI job
telemetryHooks: Hourly Merkle root anchored to public ledger
Daily WORM integrity audit + cross-region attestation
Drift + fairness + interpretability KPIs streamed to SIEM

sections.title: Capital overlay responsiveness (Basel III/IV ICAAP Pillar 2)
sections.approach: Treat AI model risk as a Pillar-2 add-on; recompute the overlay within 24h of any material change (retraining, drift breach, fairness incident, supervisor query).
sections.kpi: <= 24 hours from trigger to recomputed overlay

M3_tlosSeverity.title: M3 — Three Lines of Defense + SEV-0..SEV-3 Incident Escalation
M3_tlosSeverity.summary: Operating discipline that turns governance theory into auditable action.

lod.responsibilities: Build, operate, monitor models within risk appetite; raise issues

lod.owner: MRM + Compliance + DPO + CISO + AI Safety
lod.responsibilities: Independent challenge, validation, policy, oversight; own RAS
Audit AIMS effectiveness; audit 2nd LoD; report to Audit Committee

matrix.name: Existential / frontier breach
matrix.examples: Frontier model exfiltration; capability-gate bypass; uncontained AGI behavior
matrix.notif: Board chair + AI Safety Institute + lead supervisor + treaty authority

matrix.name: Critical regulatory or systemic
matrix.examples: Material adverse-action SLA breach; capital overlay breach; widespread bias incident
matrix.notif: CRO + CCO + lead supervisor (24h) + Board (next session)

matrix.name: High operational
matrix.examples: Single-tenant outage; PSI > 0.2 on protected attribute; OPA bundle drift

matrix.name: Moderate / advisory
matrix.examples: Minor model drift; documentation gap; non-blocking finding

M4_frontierSafety.title: M4 — Frontier AGI Safety & Containment
M4_frontierSafety.summary: Capability-tiered safety stack with kinetic enforcement.

tiers.name: Frontier foundation model (>=10^25 FLOPs)
tiers.gate: Frontier Capability Review Board + treaty disclosure (G7/UK AISI/EU AI Office)

tiers.name: ASI candidate
tiers.gate: Independent escrow + multi-jurisdiction co-custody + kill-switch with FROST quorum

sections.title: Containment stack
components: Constitutional AI guard model + Semantic Alignment Judge
Mechanistic interpretability (latent circuit scanner + cosine tripwires)
Immutable WORM telemetry (Kafka + S3 Object Lock 10y)
PQC-signed envelopes (Ed25519 + Dilithium3) + FROST threshold sealing

sections.title: Forbidden actions
list: Persuasion of human operators to disable safety controls
Acquisition of compute resources beyond approved budget
Modification of own training corpus or weights (unauthorised)
Coordination with other agents outside governed swarm topology

sections.title: Frontier disclosure SLA
obligations: Notify lead AI Safety Institute within 4h of capability-gate breach
File EU AI Act Art. 55 systemic-risk evaluation within 15 days
Co-sign with G7 Hiroshima Process Code of Conduct rapporteur
Convene Frontier Capability Review Board within 24h

M5_supervisoryKpis.title: M5 — Supervisory-Grade KPIs
M5_supervisoryKpis.summary: Eighteen KPIs that supervisors actually probe.

kpis.name: False-Negative Detection Rate (FNDR)
kpis.definition: Fraction of injected adversarial events not detected by monitoring

kpis.name: Cross-Jurisdictional Drift Reconciliation Time
kpis.definition: Time from divergent disclosure detection to reconciled JSOP message

kpis.name: Interpretability Coverage Ratio (ICR)
kpis.definition: % of high-risk decisions with SHAP + counterfactual stored

kpis.name: Capital Overlay Responsiveness
kpis.definition: Time from trigger to recomputed Pillar 2 AI add-on

kpis.name: Decision Traceability Coverage
kpis.definition: % of decisions reproducible from signed envelope

kpis.name: Containment MTTD
kpis.definition: Mean time to detect containment violation

kpis.name: Supervisory Query SLA p95
kpis.definition: Time to respond to supervisor probe

kpis.name: Frontier Disclosure SLA
kpis.definition: Time to notify AI Safety Institute on capability breach

M6_querySimulation.title: M6 — Regulator Query Simulation Pack & Supervisory Interrogation Scripts
M6_querySimulation.summary: Pre-rehearsed responses to the 50 most likely supervisor probes; fully scripted role-plays.

queries.prompt: Demonstrate the sensitivity of your Pillar 2 AI overlay to a 30% increase in model risk tier 1 population.
Show the 2nd LoD effective challenge documentation for the most recent Tier-1 promotion.
Provide SMF24 senior-manager attestation chain for AI-CR-UNDERWRITE-01 over the past 4 quarters.
Submit systemic-risk evaluation for FRONTIER-FM-01 under Art. 55, with red-team and interpretability evidence.
Explain a randomly selected adverse-action decision in plain language with feature attributions.
Walk through the GDPR Art. 22 human-review path for a contested decision.
Demonstrate compute budget enforcement and tripwire history for FRONTIER-FM-01.

scripts.scenario: Bias drift reconciliation across ECB + Fed + PRA
scripts.openingProbe: Reconcile your AIR reporting deltas to me in 2 sentences.
Show me 3 contested decisions and the human reviewer outcomes.
Replay the last tripwire event end-to-end including kinetic action latency.

sections.title: Drill cadence
cadence: Quarterly tabletop with rotating regulator persona
Annual joint examination drill (ECB + Fed + PRA simulated)
Surprise red-team probe (signed by CRO) twice per year

M7_blackSwan.title: M7 — Black Swan Supervisory Scenarios
M7_blackSwan.summary: Seven low-probability / high-impact scenarios with pre-staged response.

scenarios.name: Synchronised cross-bank model failure
scenarios.description: Same vendor foundation model fails simultaneously across multiple G-SIBs, triggering systemic credit freeze.
scenarios.preStagedResponse: Failover to deterministic challenger + invoke FSB Crisis Coordination + capital overlay spike

scenarios.name: Frontier model exfiltration
scenarios.description: Insider exfiltrates frontier weights via covert channel.
scenarios.preStagedResponse: FROST quorum kill-switch; treaty disclosure; PQC re-key; counterintel partnership

scenarios.name: Adversarial regulator AI
scenarios.description: Hostile state-sponsored AI generates plausible but false supervisory queries to manipulate disclosures.
scenarios.preStagedResponse: JSOP signature verification + supervisor identity attestation + freeze suspect channel

scenarios.name: Ritual collapse / Codex desynchronisation
scenarios.description: Annual Codex sealing fails due to executive turnover during seismic event.
scenarios.preStagedResponse: Continuity inscription protocol + emergency NED quorum + 90-day grace period

scenarios.name: Cross-jurisdictional drift cascade
scenarios.description: EU + US + UK supervisors interpret the same metric differently, triggering simultaneous enforcement.
scenarios.preStagedResponse: JSOP reconciliation message within 4h + capital overlay buffer + GC unified narrative

scenarios.name: AGI persuasion attack on Board
scenarios.description: Frontier model successfully crafts a persuasion campaign aimed at NEDs to disable safety controls.
scenarios.preStagedResponse: Read-only Board access mode + dual-control NED authentication + AI Safety Institute notification

scenarios.name: Quantum break of pre-PQC archive
scenarios.preStagedResponse: Re-anchor archive with PQC + supervisor co-signing + integrity restatement

sections.title: Pre-staged playbooks
sections.exerciseFrequency: Annual rotation, two scenarios per drill

M8_maturity.title: M8 — AGI Governance Maturity Model (M0..M5)
M8_maturity.summary: Six-tier maturity ladder with named capabilities and entry/exit criteria.

tiers.name: Ad hoc
tiers.capabilities: Manual reviews; no AIMS; ungoverned shadow AI
tiers.exitCriteria: Adopt AIMS scope + AI inventory v1

tiers.name: Documented
tiers.capabilities: AIMS Sections 1-5 in place; manual evidence

tiers.name: Industrialised
tiers.capabilities: Terraform + OPA enforced; CI/CD gates; >= 75% control automation
tiers.exitCriteria: RSP v2.0; SR 11-7 effective challenge live

tiers.name: Federated
tiers.capabilities: JSOP active; multi-regulator filings; predictive forecasters live
tiers.exitCriteria: RSP v2.4; joint exam passed; FNDR <= 1%

tiers.name: Autonomous (with override)
tiers.capabilities: RSP v2.6 streaming attestation; autonomous supervisory advisories accepted; Codex continuity proven
tiers.exitCriteria: Maintained for 4 consecutive quarters across 8+ supervisors

M9_commandCenter.summary: Single-pane-of-glass for Board, CRO, CAIO, CISO, and supervisors.

sections.title: Information architecture
panes: Pane D — Supervisor activity feed (queries, JSOP messages)
Pane F — Codex ritual status + next ceremony

sections.title: Interaction patterns
patterns: Click-through to evidence: every metric -> envelope -> Merkle root
Hover reveals: regulator citation overlay on every claim
Replay-from-anywhere: any UI surface can launch a deterministic replay
Supervisor read-only mode: PII redacted automatically based on SPIFFE id
Time-scrubber: scrub the dashboard back to any prior state with cryptographic proof

sections.title: Population-scale replay heatmap
sections.details: Renders up to 12M decisions as a hex-bin heatmap pivoted by feature deciles + protected attribute. Replay is deterministic: each cell links back to the signed decision envelope set.

sections.title: Predictive Governance Dashboard
sections.details: Surfaces 7-day breach forecasts (Prophet + ARIMA ensemble), control-fatigue forecasts, and regulatory-question forecasts. Each forecast pre-stages a remediation PR for Board review.

M10_codexAutoUpdater.title: M10 — Codex Auto-Updater Flow & Supervisory Narrative
M10_codexAutoUpdater.summary: How the Codex updates itself from telemetry and emits an explainable supervisory narrative.

sections.title: Auto-update flow
stages: Watch: telemetry topics + Codex git mirror
Diff: detect material change vs. last sealed Codex
Compose: generate human-readable narrative (LLM grounded on evidence)
Validate: Legal + GC sign-off via two-key approval
Sign: Ed25519 + Dilithium3 + FROST quorum if Codex chapter sealed
Inscribe: append to Resonance Archive with Merkle anchor
Broadcast: push update to Supervisor Feed + Board pack

sections.title: Supervisory narrative template
sections.skeleton:
<title>Codex Update — {date}</title>
<abstract>Material AI risk posture changes since last sealing, with regulator implications.</abstract>
<content>1. Material control changes
2. KPI movement (K1..K18)
3. Frontier capability status
4. Cross-jurisdictional reconciliation
5. Codex inscriptions added
6. Supervisory implications + recommended actions
7. Forward outlook (predictive governance)</content>

sections.title: Explainability principles
principles: Every claim cites an evidence record
Every metric movement explains its driver
Every regulator-relevant change cites the obligation
Every Codex inscription names its custodians

M11_briefingPlaybook.title: M11 — Interactive Board Briefing Wireframes & Supervisory Session Playbook
M11_briefingPlaybook.summary: Run the room. Every minute accountable.

screens.content: K1..K18 strip + Sev incidents + frontier tier status
Ritual schedule + custodian quorum + inscription preview

sections.title: Supervisory session playbook
stages: T-7 days: confirm scope + share JSOP slice
T-0 minute 0: Codex chapter intro + custodian roll-call
T-0 minute 5: live KPI walk + replay sample
T+1 day: signed minutes inscribed in Resonance Archive
T+5 days: post-session JSOP message + remediation PR (if any)

sections.title: Tone & truthfulness
principles: Concede known gaps; show remediation timestamps
Cite evidence; never assert without an envelope
Honour silence: let the room think

M12_supervisoryApi.title: M12 — Supervisory API Reference Blueprint & Trust Contract

sections.title: API blueprint
sections.auth: mTLS + supervisor SPIFFE id + per-call OPA policy

sections.title: Trust contract
clauses: Truthfulness: every response signed; misrepresentation = breach
Reproducibility: any reply can be re-derived from telemetry
Privacy: PII redaction applied per supervisor scope
Continuity: contract survives executive turnover via Codex
Mutual attestation: supervisor identity also attested
Right to revoke: institution may pause federation with notice
Right to challenge: supervisor may probe with counterfactuals

sections.title: Trust contract lifecycle
stages: Sign: institution Board + supervisor authorised signatory
Renew: annually or on regulatory change

M13_trustDashboardJsop.title: M13 — Supervisory Trust Dashboard & Joint Supervisory Operating Protocol (JSOP)
M13_trustDashboardJsop.summary: Multi-supervisor situational awareness + an interoperability protocol.

sections.title: Supervisory Trust Dashboard
metrics: Per-supervisor trust score (replies, attestations, query frequency)
Disclosure freshness (time since last RSP slice)

sections.title: JSOP — Joint Supervisory Operating Protocol
sections.purpose: Allow ECB + Fed + PRA + others to operate as a coordinated examination cohort with shared queries, scoped disclosures, and reconciled findings.
messageOps: Disclose: scoped artefact share with consent metadata
sections.transport: mTLS + SPIFFE + JSON-LD over HTTP/2 or NATS

sections.title: Joint examination ritual
agenda: Codex chapter intro by institution custodians

M14_codexCharter.title: M14 — Supervisory Codex Charter: Sealing, Renewal, Continuity, Inscription, Resonance Archives
M14_codexCharter.summary: Cultural persistence layer that ensures the institution's AI risk posture survives executive turnover, regulator change, model regeneration, and seismic events. The Codex is the explicit memory of governance.

sections.title: Codex structure
elements: Preamble — the institution's covenant on AI
Chapters — one per fiscal year, per material change
Resonance Archive — multi-modal evidence corpus (text, telemetry, video, ceremony recording)
Custodian roster — humans accountable for each ritual
Continuity binder — instructions for emergency continuation

rituals.name: Sealing
rituals.trigger: Annual + on Sev-0 + on major regulatory change
rituals.actors: Board chair, CEO, CRO, CAIO, CISO, CCO, DPO, GC, External Ethicist

rituals.name: Renewal
rituals.actors: Same as sealing + new custodians as needed

rituals.name: Continuity
rituals.trigger: Executive turnover, seismic event, supervisor change

rituals.name: Inscription
rituals.artefact: Signed inscription appended to Resonance Archive

sections.title: Multi-modal evidence integrity
modalities: Artefact: model weights digest + SBOM + in-toto
Ceremony: video recording with NTP-anchored timestamps
Ritual: choreographed sequence of human + machine actions
sections.integrityModel: All modalities reduced to a content hash; hashes form a chapter-level Merkle tree; chapter root anchored to public ledger; FROST threshold signature held jointly by Board, CRO, CAIO, CISO, CCO, DPO, GC, ethicist.

sections.title: Self-verifying, temporally continuous governance
properties: Self-verifying: the Codex can prove its own integrity in O(log n)
Temporally continuous: chapter chain spans executive turnover
Regulator-integrated: cohort supervisors witness and co-sign
Culturally persistent: rituals re-affirm posture beyond individuals
Multi-modal: text + telemetry + artefact + attestation + ceremony
sections.boardCovenant: We, the Board, commit that AI systems operating in our name remain truthful, auditable, contained, and subordinate to human flourishing — across executives, across regulators, across regenerations of model and method.

kpiGaugeReact.purpose: Animated radial KPI gauge component (React + SVG)

deterministicAuditReplayReact.purpose: Deterministic audit replay with side-by-side diff

populationReplayHeatmapPy.code:
import numpy as np
import pandas as pd

def population_heatmap(envelopes_df, protected_col, score_col, n_bins=10):
    # envelopes_df: rows = signed decision envelopes
    df = envelopes_df.copy()
    df['decile'] = pd.qcut(df[score_col], q=n_bins,
                           labels=False, duplicates='drop')
    grid = (df.groupby([protected_col, 'decile']).size()
              .unstack(fill_value=0))
    rates = grid.div(grid.sum(axis=1), axis=0)
    air = rates.min().min() / max(rates.max().max(), 1e-9)
    return {"grid": grid.to_dict(), "rates": rates.to_dict(), "air_min": float(air)}
